name: Foix Reservoir Loop Trail
name_original: Pantà de Foix y Castell de Castellet – Parc del Foix – Ruta Circular
description: Hike a moderate 7.0-mile (11.3 km) circular route in Parc del Foix, exploring the historic Castellet Castle and scenic Foix Reservoir.
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Parc Del Foix are found within a diverse natural park spanning over 3,100 hectares in the Alt Penedès region. The terrain transitions from the Penedès plain to a more mountainous and wooded area, part of the Garraf massif. The centerpiece is the Foix Reservoir, which creates a significant wetland environment, surrounded by holm oak and white pine forests. The park's varied ecosystems support a rich array of flora and fauna, making it a notable destination for outdoor activities. Best family friendly hiking trails in Parc Del Foix The most popular family friendly hiking route is Foix Reservoir Loop Trail, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views of the reservoir and passes by the historic Castellet Castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Foix Reservoir – Foix Reservoir loop from Pantà de Foix, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This trail provides an immersive experience around the reservoir, showcasing the park's aquatic and wooded landscapes. Local hikers also love the La Font d'Hort Trailhead – Font d'Horta loop from Masia la Creu, a 2.6 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through dense forest, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Hiking in Parc Del Foix is defined by reservoir loops, varied forest paths, and historical sites like Castellet Castle. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several easy and moderate family friendly hiking trails. The routes in Parc Del Foix are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 1,100 reviews. More than 7,500 hikers have used komoot to explore Parc Del Foix's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are there in Parc Del Foix?
A: Parc Del Foix offers a good selection of family-friendly hiking trails. Our guide features 13 routes, with 4 categorized as easy and 8 as moderate, ensuring options for various family fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with family in Parc Del Foix?
A: The park is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for family hikes, avoiding the summer heat. These seasons also showcase the diverse flora, from blooming shrubs to vibrant autumn leaves. Winter can also be beautiful, especially around the reservoir.

Q: Are there easy, short hikes suitable for young children?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes perfect for younger children. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31735692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir loop from Parc del Foix&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km trail with minimal elevation gain, offering a gentle introduction to the park's beauty.

Q: What interesting sights can we see along family-friendly trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4019675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a crucial wetland area. You can also spot historical landmarks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1415270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellet Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a picturesque backdrop to many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5733267" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cal Bladet Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is another popular spot for panoramic views.

Q: Are there circular routes suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the family-friendly trails in Parc Del Foix are circular, making them convenient for day trips. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19831623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Font d'Hort Trailhead loop from Masia la Creu&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 4.25 km route that immerses you in the park's forests.

Q: Can we spot any wildlife during our hike?
A: Parc Del Foix is rich in wildlife, especially around the reservoir. It's a significant spot for birdwatching, with over 150 cataloged bird species. You might also encounter aquatic animals like turtles and various water snakes, and if you're lucky, mammals such as foxes or wild boars in the wooded areas.

Q: Is there parking available for family hikes?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at key trailheads and access points around Parc Del Foix, particularly near popular starting points for reservoir loops and castle visits. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route pages.

Q: Can we reach Parc Del Foix by public transport?
A: While less direct than by car, it is possible to reach areas near Parc Del Foix by public transport. Bus routes serve some of the surrounding towns like Castellet i la Gornal. For detailed planning, it's advisable to check local bus schedules to determine the best access points for your chosen trail.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the trails?
A: Parc Del Foix is generally dog-friendly, but it's important to keep dogs on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all visitors. Always clean up after your pet.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in Parc Del Foix?
A: The trails in Parc Del Foix are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil natural escape, the scenic views of the Foix Reservoir, and the historical charm of sites like Castellet Castle, making it a perfect blend of nature and culture for families.

Q: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trails?
A: While the park itself is a natural area, you can find cafes and restaurants in the nearby villages such as Castellet i la Gornal and Santa Margarida i els Monjos. These towns offer options for a meal or a snack before or after your family hike.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in Parc Del Foix?
A: The terrain in Parc Del Foix is varied, ranging from flat paths along the reservoir to gently rolling hills through wooded areas. You'll encounter a mix of dirt trails, some rocky sections, and shaded paths through holm oak and pine forests. Most family-friendly routes are well-maintained and suitable for sturdy walking shoes.
tips:
Castellet Castle dates from the 10th century, originally little more than a large fortified house with a watchtower, it was intended to protect the Hispanic March of al-Andalus.

Over time and the successive occupants it did not fall into disuse thanks to its strategic position between Garraf and Penedès. Today it is a tourist attraction, thanks to its state of conservation and its privileged location on the meander of the Foix River, today occupied by the Foix reservoir. There is a terrace bar, very popular with bikers and cyclists, just off the road.
Views of the Foix from Castellet

Located on the Sierra Mala, this abandoned farmhouse is another example of this type of construction in the area, next to the Casa Alta or Cal Cremat, now uninhabited due to the lack of water and the consequent drop in agricultural productivity.

The pantà de Foix was inaugurated in 1928 and was built to supply irrigation water to the orchards. It collects the waters of the river Foix, which flows into Cubelles. You can also see waterfowl from an observatory in front of the Foix reservoir. Don't forget to take your binoculars.

This old farmhouse on the banks of the Foix reservoir is made up of numerous outbuildings and corrals. It is surrounded by a bastion or wall, which served to protect property from bandits.

A few meters from the farmhouse is the Cal Bladet viewpoint, from where you can observe the diversity of fauna and flora. It is a place very frequented by lovers of ornithology, since in addition to the typical birds of the riverside forest, aquatic birds are also observed.
There are great views from here.
Really nice and shady place to rest for a while. However, lately there has been no water here.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Pantà de Foix and Castell de Castellet – Parc del Foix – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate. While generally accessible, it requires a good physical condition due to its length and some sections with small stairs. It might be tiring for children not accustomed to hiking, though shorter, easier sections are available around the reservoir for families and beginners.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, from thick, wild forests, especially near Font d'Horta, to sections bordering the Foix Reservoir. You'll pass through white pine and oak forests, with olive, elm, ash, poplar, and willow trees near the water. In autumn, the landscape is particularly vibrant with ochre and reddish hues among the park's cliffs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Pantà de Foix and Castell de Castellet circular route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Castellet Castle, offering wonderful views and a glimpse into medieval history. You'll also enjoy picturesque views of the Foix Reservoir from various viewpoints like View of the Foix Reservoir and Cal Bladet and the Foix Reservoir Viewpoint. The route also passes by the Ruins of Cal Balaguer and the natural monument Font d'Horta.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Pantà de Foix and Castell de Castellet circular route?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from the village of Castellet, which is a common access point for the Parc del Foix. Parking is usually available within or near the village, but it's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, as specific parking areas can fill up.

faqs.question: Can I reach the Parc del Foix trail by public transport?
faqs.answer: While the Parc del Foix is accessible, public transport options directly to the trailhead in Castellet might be limited. It's recommended to check local bus schedules from nearby towns like Vilanova i la Geltrú or Vilafranca del Penedès, as connections can vary. Driving is generally the most convenient option.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Parc del Foix?
faqs.answer: No, there are generally no permits or entrance fees required to hike this route or access the Parc del Foix. The park is open to the public for recreational activities.

faqs.question: Is the Pantà de Foix and Castell de Castellet circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it is important to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near the reservoir, to protect the local ecosystem and ensure the safety of other hikers and your pet. Always clean up after your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly recommended. In spring, the vegetation is lush, and temperatures are pleasant. Autumn offers stunning foliage with the ochre and reddish hues of poplars and holm oaks. Summers can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winters are generally mild.

faqs.question: Does this circular route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that are part of the Garraf (44%), Penedès 360 Trekking (35%), and Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies) (33%) routes. Other smaller sections include Variant: Desembocadura del Foix - Castell de Queralt, La Font d'Horta, and Penyafort-Castellet.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I expect to see around the Foix Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The Foix Reservoir is a vital ecological refuge, especially for aquatic birds and amphibians. You might spot various bird species, particularly from the adapted wildlife observatory along the route. The surrounding forests are also home to typical Mediterranean fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is mostly natural, the village of Castellet, where the route often starts and ends, offers some amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and potentially restrooms in Castellet. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of the hike.

waypoints.name: Cal Bladet and the Foix Reservoir Viewpoint
